channel.title: Problem-Solving Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare
channel.description: Recent content in Problem-Solving Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare

item.title: Code for AVL trees
item.description: Supporting Python file for Recitation 4 on code for AVL trees (uses the BST code from R3).

item.title: Code for binary search trees
item.description: Supporting Python file for Recitation 3 on code for binary search trees augmented with subtree size.

item.title: Code for breadth-first search
item.description: Supporting Python file for Recitation 11 on code for breadth-first search.

item.title: Code for depth-first search
item.description: Supporting Python file for Recitation 11 on code for depth-first search.

item.title: Longest zig-zag subsequence
item.description: Live Python Coding problems (problem statement, tests, and solutions are included) for Recitation 21 on longest zig-zag subsequence. (This zip file includes: 3 .py files, 1 .pyc file, 1 .rb file, and 6 data files.)

item.title: Matrix chain multiplication
item.description: Live Python Coding problems (problem statement, tests, and solutions are included) for Recitation 21 on matrix chain multiplication. (This zip file includes: 3 .py files, 1 .pyc file, 1 .rb file, and 6 data files.)

item.title: Mutable dictionary keys
item.description: Supporting Python file for Recitation 5 on the dangers of mutable dictionary keys.

item.title: Open addressing code
item.description: Support Python file for Recitation 7 on open addressing code.

item.title: Recitation 1
item.description: Recitation slides on document distance in Python (docdist{1,2,3,4}.py).

item.title: Recitation 11
item.description: Recitation slides on breadth-first search and depth-first search.

item.title: Recitation 12
item.description: Recitation slides on two-way BFS, stable sorting, and DRY.

item.title: Recitation 14
item.description: Recitation slides on Dijkstra (pseudocode, preconditions, examples, why it works) and priority queues (review, extended Python implementation).

item.title: Recitation 16
item.description: Recitation slides on dynamic programming: big ideas, memoization in Fibonacci, crazy cards, Dijkstra, and Bellman-Ford algorithm.

item.title: Recitation 17
item.description: Recitation slides on dynamic programming: beating Super Mario Brothers, getting points back on tests (LCS), and Crazy Eights.

item.title: Recitation 18
item.description: Recitation slides on dynamic programming: maximum-sum sub-array and Tetris.

item.title: Recitation 2
item.description: Recitation slides on the Python cost model, asymptotic notation, and mergesort.

item.title: Recitation 23
item.description: Recitation slides with exam review questions and concluding discussion for the course.

item.title: Recitation 3
item.description: Recitation slides on binary search trees.

item.title: Recitation 4
item.description: Recitation slides on AVL Trees (balanced binary search trees).

item.title: Recitation 5
item.description: Recitation slides on hashing in Python and mutability.

item.title: Recitation 6
item.description: Recitation slides on Karp-Rabin, rolling hashes principles, and code.

item.title: Recitation 7
item.description: Recitation slides on hashing, open addressing, theory review, Python code, and rolling hashes.

item.title: Recitation 8
item.description: Recitation slides with an overview of sorting methods and discussion of heaps as data structures.

item.title: Recitation 9
item.description: Recitation slides containing quiz review problems.

item.title: Rolling hash code
item.description: Support Python file for Recitation 6 on rolling hash code.

item.title: Zipped data (numbers)
item.description: Supporting files for Recitation 2. (This zip file includes: 2 .jpg files, 1 .gz file, 1 .tiff file, 1 .numbers file, and 1 .pkginfo file.)
